# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Bash script for deploying to AWS using the AWS CLI. The script should authenticate with AWS and perform deployment operations. Ensure the script can be used safely in both local development and CI/CD environments.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

#!/bin/sh -eux # this ensures that we catch errors as soon as possible, including those resulting from external APIs or other systems such as the network. Also makes bash easier to read than using 'set -e' (it can be combined with '-x') because it will cause a trace of what’s going on when an error occurs
# you may also want shell_login for your production environment, which is more secure in AWS – ensure not disclose this data as any public info. Use the IAM role that has sufficient permissions to create ECR repositories and deploy applications if required by EC2 Instance profile or User Data Runner script
# replace these values with real ones at runtime using parameter substitution - for example, ${parameter_name:-default} where default is used when '$parameter' variable not defined. 
awsAccessKeyID=$(printf '%s\n' "your-accesskey" | openssl sha1 -binary | openssl base64) # add your AWS Access Key here in the format of $AWS_ACCESS_KEY (without quotes), it must be used within scripts or environment variables
awsSecretAccessKey=$(printf '%s\n' "your-secretkey" | openssl sha1 -binary |openssl base64) # add your AWS Secret Access Key here in the format of $AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY (without quotes), it must be used within scripts or environment variables
awsDefaultRegion=$(printf '%s\n' "your-region"  | openssl sha1 -binary |openssl base64) # add your AWS Default Region here in the format of $AWS_DEFAULT_REGION, e.g., uswest2 (without quotes), it must be used within scripts or environment variables
# create ECR Repository and build Docker image then push to Amazon ECR using Fargate & CodeStar actions if needed  
aws ecr get-login --region $AWS_DEFAULT_REGION | /bin/sh -c "docker login --username AWS --password '$awsAccessKeyID'" # replace with your real access key and password  (must be used within scripts or environment variables)
# Create ECR Repository to store Docker images if not exists. Replace <repository_name>, it's just a name here that you want for the repository on AWS ecr which stores all of your docker image as well configuration settings like registry login and authentication details etc  will be automatically stored in CI/CD setting
aws ecr create-repository --repository-name "<yourrepoNameHere>" --region $AWS_DEFAULT_REGION || true # replace <your repo name here>  to the actual repository you want, ensure that your AWS account has sufficient permissions and access rights.  A `true` at end of command is used in case when ECR Repository already exists
# Then it can be done by running a shell script with all necessary commands if needed like docker build or any other required task using environment variable for the repository URL to store Docker images within Amazon Elastic Container Registry.  If you want AWS CI/CD runner is used, then replace <command> in below code line of your .github\workflows yml file
# It must contain commands like `aws ecr get-login --region $AWS_DEFAULT_REGION | /bin/sh -c "docker login ..."` and more. These would be passed as environment variables to the script via Github Actions or any other CI tool if required (to use it with AWS Codestar actions)
aws ecr get-login --region $AWS_DEFAULT_REGION | /bin/sh -c "$command" # replace command in above line  and make sure to add new environment variable for this. It's needed when the script needs any Docker image build or anything related operation that depends on docker
